mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress consumer spending expectations and drive negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4pp in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 55% of months where UNRATE increases exceed 0.4 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth that supports corporate earnings and produces positive quarterly equity returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both show positive month-over-month growth
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (win rate < 45%) over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production trigger immediate risk-off positioning as manufacturers cut output, causing negative short-term S&P 500 returns during economic slowdowns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth does not precede negative 5-day returns during contraction periods (correlation > -0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, higher borrowing costs compress corporate profit margins and equity valuations, generating negative forward returns for the S&P 500.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 60% of observations where FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening that boosts wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive quarterly equity returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases while UNRATE decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations, producing positive short-term S&P 500 returns as markets price in stronger earnings.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no predictive power for 5-day forward returns (t-statistic < 1.5) across all available data.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals healthy capacity utilization that supports positive monthly S&P 500 returns during low inflation periods.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth during low inflation periods fails to generate positive 20-day returns (win rate < 40%).
